name: GitHub Copilot CLI Research
observations: AI-powered data validation can improve penny item accuracy
Drag-and-drop interfaces enhance user experience for list management
Automated freshness checks keep penny data current without manual intervention
Custom agents can generate location-specific hunting strategies
Enhanced authentication enables personalization features
Community features increase engagement and create network effects

name: PennyCentral Data Validation Patterns
observations: Strong SKU validation system in lib/sku.ts with Zod schema
Sophisticated item name quality assessment in lib/item-name-quality.ts
Code duplication in validation logic across components
Need for centralized validation hook

name: PennyCentral User Experience Components
observations: Well-structured penny list table with accessibility features
Good keyboard navigation and responsive design
Form validation is good but inconsistent across components
Need for unified form validation system

name: PennyCentral Authentication System
observations: Minimal OTP-based authentication using Supabase
Basic functionality but limited personalization features
Graceful fallback when auth not configured
Opportunity for social login integration

name: PennyCentral AI/Automation Integration
observations: Robust backend automation with multiple enrichment scripts
Limited integration with user-facing features
Scripts include auto-enrich.ts, serpapi-enrich.ts, staging-warmer.py
Need for user-facing automation controls and status indicators

name: Next.js App Router Data Patterns
observations: PennyCentral uses Server Components effectively with 5-minute revalidate
Tagged caching with unstable_cache is top priority for improvement
Current pagination approach is optimal for SEO
Suspense boundaries could improve perceived performance
Server Actions needed for optimistic UI updates

name: Caching Strategies for Large Datasets
observations: unstable_cache with tags provides granular cache control
revalidateTag allows targeted cache invalidation
cacheLife profiles useful for different content types
Current time-based revalidate is basic but functional

name: Pagination vs Infinite Scroll
observations: Pagination is better for SEO and bookmarkability
Current implementation already follows best practices
Hybrid approach with 'Load More' button possible
Infinite scroll not recommended for this use case

name: Supabase RLS Patterns for Community Features
observations: PennyCentral needs RLS patterns for community features including user profiles, list sharing, comments, and social features
Current implementation has basic RLS for user lists and penny list submissions
Performance optimization critical: avoid joins in RLS policies, use IN clauses, wrap auth.uid() in SELECT
User profiles should have public/private privacy controls with RLS policies
Comment system should inherit visibility from parent list permissions
Follow relationships use associative table pattern with composite primary keys

name: PennyCentral Community Feature Implementation
observations: Implementation sequence: 1) User Profiles, 2) Enhanced List Sharing, 3) Comment System, 4) Performance Optimization, 5) Follow System, 6) Multi-tenant Patterns, 7) Reputation System
User profiles are P1 priority and foundation for all community features
Enhanced list sharing builds on existing token-based system in list_shares table
Performance optimization needed as RLS complexity increases with community features

name: Supabase RLS Performance Best Practices
observations: Use IN clauses instead of joins in RLS policies for 99% performance improvement
Wrap auth.uid() in SELECT to cache result: (select auth.uid()) = user_id
Index all columns used in RLS policy filters
Consider materialized views for complex permission hierarchies
Force RLS even for table owners with: alter table table_name force row level security
